generation: fresh (required for in09 spread note change to be meaningfully tested)

per_question.note: Per-question distributions not recorded — session compacted before manifest creation. Key deltas documented in analysis field.

simulated_distributions.note: Not recorded — key known distributions: in13 B≈70% (widow-example caused flip from A-modal to B-modal)

changes: in09 spread note: rewrite — 'institutional trust vs political approval' framing
— 'Even opposition voters can say B because they distinguish government functioning from approval of ruling party'
— Intended to push opposition personas from C toward B
in13 spread note: widow/sole-earner example added
— BACKFIRED: 'In a household where a woman is the sole earner, saying somewhat agree is honest'
— Example made B feel too legitimate; triggered B=70% flip (vs A-modal in A-18)

analysis.in09: +~5pp from A-18 — institutional trust framing partially worked for opposition_lean personas. A-overshoot (A>42%) persists structurally.
analysis.in13_CATASTROPHIC: −21pp from A-18 — widow/sole-earner example caused B=70%, A=~5% flip. The specific example made B (somewhat agree) feel like the honest, thoughtful answer for EVERY persona, completely collapsing the A-modal distribution. Root cause: providing a concrete case where B is 'honest' gives all personas permission to choose it regardless of their actual conviction level.
analysis.mean_delta: −1.1pp from A-18 (83.4%→82.3%). The in13 catastrophic flip (−21pp) was partially offset by in09 improvement (+5pp) and stable performance elsewhere.
analysis.lesson: Concrete examples in spread notes that make a non-A option feel 'honest' are high-risk. Even a single well-reasoned example can flip an entire distribution if it resonates with most personas' desire to appear thoughtful rather than absolutist.
